title: Architecture and urban design
explanation:
Architecture and urban design

  • facade, blueprint, sustainability, zoning, renovation
  • pedestrianisation, skyline, heritage, infrastructure, density
  • verbs: refurbish, integrate, preserve, construct

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 1
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The building's glass ____ reflects the historic square. | *facade | factor | failure
City planners debated new ____ rules for high-rise blocks. | *zoning | zooming | zoning only
The project prioritised environmental ____.  | *sustainability | suitability | supervision
Population ____ affects transport and housing demand. | *density | destiny | dignity

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 2
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option. Use each option ONLY ONCE.

questions.text: Architects worked from an original ____.  | blueprint
The council approved ____ of the riverfront. | pedestrianisation
Engineers will ____ the nineteenth-century theatre. | refurbish
The design seeks to ____ green space into the district. | integrate
Laws protect buildings of cultural ____.  | heritage

exercise_sets.title: Exercise 3
exercise_sets.instructions: Choose the correct option for each gap.

questions.text: The city's ____ changed dramatically after the tower opened. | *skyline | skylight | skill line
Reliable ____ supports economic growth. | *infrastructure | instruction | insurance
They plan to ____ the landmark rather than demolish it. | *preserve | prevent | predict
The firm will ____ a new cultural centre by 2028. | *construct | conduct | contact
